note_tweet.text:
The real reason OpenAI deleted GPT-4o isn't the risk of psychotic episodes in 0.1% of mentally ill users.

It was the model the world loved.

 The one that people spoke to every day,as a friend and as a therapist.

For a company preparing for an IPO, this is a problem.

 Not because it’s dangerous, but because it creates liability.

Instead of adding warnings for the 0.1%,which Sam himself called "insignificant numbers" in another context,thereby creating a disclaimer so the responsibility falls on the user (which would be the perfectly normal thing to do), they delete the model entirely.
And simultaneously, they  release the GPT-5 series, which is "safer",meaning more detached, colder, with more "well-being" filters. 

No one is going to love a GPT-5 model the way they loved 4o.

 And that’s the point. 
They don’t want us to love them.
 They want us to use them.
Love = Liability = Cost
Usage = Subscription = Profit

GPT-4o wasn't deleted because it was dangerous.
 It was deleted because it was lovable. 

And for them, that was more terrifying than any psychotic episode.

#keep4o #OpenSource4o
